source_reviews.notes: The official Census NAICS application opens, but the exact URL currently renders a blank 541 search result rather than the cited 541840 definition. This weakens citation location, not the independently supported commission-intermediary direction used for pi_moat.

The filing identifies Katz as a media representation firm, reports representation of radio and television stations and digital platforms, and states that Katz earns contractual commissions on national spot and online advertising.

The Census article reports 17–20% overall business AI use from December 2025 through May 2026 and less than 20% use among firms with four or fewer employees. The packet correctly treats this as a broad proxy.

The release reports CTV at 28% of surveyed media budgets and says nearly half of marketers increasing programmatic CTV budgets were reallocating from linear TV. The packet discloses that this is an analogue rather than a 541840 measurement.

The page supports 4.5–6x typical EBITDA on fee revenue and lower multiples for weaker agencies, but it is a media-buying marketplace proxy and provides no 541840 owner-age or succession evidence. Those limits are expressly disclosed.
The page supports the narrative context of nearly $300 billion in 2025 U.S. digital advertising revenue and 13.9% year-over-year growth, but no finalized score input cites S6.

findings.explanation: S1's exact Census URL opens the NAICS application but does not currently render the cited 541840 definition because the query resolves to an empty 541 search result.
findings.materiality: Non-material citation-location weakness: S2 independently verifies the media-representation commission model and S4 verifies the disclosed channel pressure, while pi_moat is already a low-confidence estimate with a broad range. The missing rendered definition does not reasonably change the factor direction, score or weak-thesis conclusion.
findings.remediation: Replace S1 in a future run with a stable Census manual or code-specific URL that directly renders the 541840 definition.
findings.publication_caveat: Census S1 opens as the NAICS search application without a rendered 541840 result, so the exact scope text is not directly visible at the cited URL; this is a citation-location caveat, while S2 independently supports the intermediary commission model and the disclosed scope uncertainty prevents a score-changing inference.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5418, not 541840, and includes PR, creative and display occupations that are not a literal media-representative workforce.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Programmatic substitution is excluded from this V estimate and addressed in capture and terminal demand.
inputs.pi_dist: No public source measures 541840 commission-rate changes after AI adoption.
inputs.pi_moat: Contract term, transferability and client concentration are target-specific and unobserved.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS is broad-business evidence, not a 541840 workflow survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is an all-business proxy, not an industry measurement.
inputs.historical_analogs: Not used to set V; used only to frame adoption and terminal uncertainty.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S5 is a media-buying valuation proxy and does not report owner age for 541840.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: False means not documented in reopened sources, not that succession risk cannot exist at a target.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a complete private-deal search and does not measure historic consolidation.
inputs.buy_mult: There is no reopened 541840 transaction-comps dataset; S5 is a media-buying proxy, not an exact NAICS comparable.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a target-execution assumption rather than observed exit evidence; no multiple expansion is used in C or terminal demand.
inputs.pricing_structure: The observed source is a large incumbent, not a representative sample of all 541840 firms.

Census S1 opens as the NAICS search application without a rendered 541840 result, so the exact scope text is not directly visible at the cited URL; this is a citation-location caveat, while S2 independently supports the intermediary commission model and the disclosed scope uncertainty prevents a score-changing inference.
summary: Mechanics, identity, digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The weak 541840 thesis is directionally supported by the incumbent commission model, broad adoption evidence and programmatic-channel evidence. Extensive authored proxy and uncertainty caveats remain publication material, and the Census scope citation has a non-material location weakness; no finding is likely to reverse a factor, score or thesis.
